ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03302051 represents a single churro. This record is a multi-piece sharing set (20 churros, two varieties, with sauces); closest_parent applied as no exact set-meal code exists.
ontology.foodex2_description: Doughnuts and similar fried dough products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers fried dough products broadly; no specific churros sharing-set code exists in FoodEx2. Closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to a churros sharing platter. SNOMED CT does not index commercial dessert sets at this granularity.

serving.description: 1 Funbox (20 churros with sauces, half of the 2-box deal)
serving.natural_language_portion: One Funbox contains approximately 20 churros (a mix of 10 classic and 10 krispy varieties) plus 6 dipping sauces and toppings, estimated at approximately 350 g per box based on typical churro portion weights from USDA reference data.

nutrient_source.title: Churros, plain (fried dough, sugar-coated) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #18617 and USDA Foundation Foods composite
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Singapore restaurant churros sharing-set entry exists in HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for churros (plain, fried, sugar-coated, ~100 g reference). Sauce contribution is estimated as a minor additive component; the dominant macronutrient profile is driven by the churro dough itself. Values represent a weighted average of classic and krispy churro types, both of which are fried wheat-dough products with sugar coating. This is an estimated adaptation; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried dough / doughnuts and similar wheat-flour fried pastry products — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for churros exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 76 is estimated by analogy to fried wheat-flour dough products (doughnuts, GI ~76, glucose reference) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables. Churros share the same base composition: refined wheat flour, deep-fried, sugar-coated, high glycemic carbohydrate density. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (76 × 169.4) / 100 ≈ 128.7, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 48.8 × 3.5 = 170.8 g (rounded to 169.4 g net after fibre adjustment). High GL reflects the large sharing-set portion size.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~290 mg/100 g) and high energy density from deep-frying
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit churros consumption to a small occasional portion, as the sodium from dough and sauces combined with high caloric density can contribute to excess sodium intake and weight gain, both of which elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~76) and very high glycemic load per sharing-set serving (~128.7)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit churros sharing sets, as the combination of refined wheat flour, deep-frying, and sugar coating produces a high GI and extremely high GL per serving, causing rapid and sustained postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (~19.4 g/100 g), saturated fat (~4.1 g/100 g), and trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) from deep-frying in vegetable oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat churros as an occasional indulgence only, as the high saturated and trans fat content from deep-frying, combined with the large sharing-set portion, significantly increases dietary fat load and may adversely affect LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Churros Funbox is a large sharing dessert set sold by Churros Republic at Funan Mall in Singapore, offered as a promotional bundle of two boxes for SGD 39 (usual price SGD 45). Each Funbox contains 20 churros — 10 classic churros and 10 krispy churros — accompanied by 6 dipping sauces and toppings. Classic churros are the traditional Spanish fried-dough pastry: a piped wheat-flour dough deep-fried until golden and coated in cinnamon sugar, yielding a crisp exterior and soft, slightly chewy interior. Krispy churros are a variant with a lighter, crispier texture achieved through a modified dough formulation or frying technique. Churros originated in Spain and Portugal and are widely consumed across Latin America and increasingly in Southeast Asia as a dessert or snack. Nutritionally, churros are energy-dense fried pastries: per 100 g they provide approximately 387 kcal, 19.4 g fat, 48.8 g carbohydrate, and 4.9 g protein. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 76 (high, analogous to fried doughnuts), and the glycemic load for a full Funbox serving (~350 g) is very high at approximately 128.7. This makes the Funbox a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease. The sodium content (~290 mg/100 g) is moderate and warrants attention for those with hypertension. The sauces (chocolate, caramel, and others) add additional sugar and fat. This product is suitable for occasional indulgence in healthy adults but is not appropriate for regular consumption by individuals with metabolic or cardiovascular conditions.
common_user_queries: How many churros are in the Churros Republic Funbox?

confidence_reason: No exact churros sharing-set entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy churros reference data (plain, fried, sugar-coated). GI is estimated by analogy to fried doughnuts from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008. Serving size is estimated from typical churro weights. Confidence is medium due to the adapted nutrient source and analogous GI estimate.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy churros data; no Singapore-specific laboratory analysis is available for this product. 2. GI of 76 is an analogous estimate based on fried wheat-flour dough products (doughnuts) per Atkinson et al. 2008; a direct GI measurement for churros is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database. 3. Serving size of 350 g per Funbox is estimated; actual weight may vary by restaurant preparation. 4. Sauce contribution to macronutrients is minor relative to the churro dough and has been incorporated into the sugar_g estimate. 5. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy for plain churros (no egg in standard formulation); this is confirmed zero per the cited source. 6. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as fried wheat-dough pastry contains no ascorbic acid. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. 8. vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, selenium_mcg are -1 as they are not reported in the USDA SR Legacy churros entry. 9. Halal status is not confirmed from the input data; is_halal is set to false pending verification. 10. The promotional price of SGD 39 for 2 boxes (U.P. SGD 45) is preserved from the field survey source record for commercial context only and does not affect nutritional values. 11. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy fat composition data for fried dough products.
